BundleInfo.name: ChimeraX-MDSmooth
BundleInfo.Author: Liam Kozma
BundleInfo.Synopsis: Reduce an MD trajectory to its meaningful frames and build clean morph movies
BundleInfo.Description:
Turn a noisy molecular-dynamics trajectory into a clean morph movie that shows real conformational motion instead of thermal jitter.
MDSmooth reads a 1D signal from the trajectory (the RMSD to a reference frame by default, or a collective motion such as PC1, the slowest tICA component IC1, or a dihedral-based dPC1), applies a zero-phase Butterworth low-pass filter to remove high-frequency jitter, keeps the frames where the signal turns (its local maxima and minima), and builds a morph between them. Per-segment step counts are chosen so the morph preserves the trajectory's real timing.
Interpolation defaults to ChimeraX's own corkscrew morph engine, so rotating domains follow a screw motion, with a dependency-free linear fallback. A kinetic mode is also available: it groups the trajectory into metastable states (tICA, a Markov State Model, then PCCA+) and tours one representative frame per state. An interactive plot shows the raw and filtered signal with the kept frames marked. Click it to scrub the trajectory, or add frames the filter would otherwise skip.
Works from the mdsmooth command or a Tools, Trajectory Analysis panel, and turns a full trajectory into a ready-to-record morph of its meaningful frames in a single step.

Classifiers.ChimeraXClassifier: ChimeraX :: Command :: mdsmooth :: Trajectory Analysis :: Low-pass filter a trajectory's RMSD and morph its significant frames
ChimeraX :: Command :: mdsmooth installLearnedCV :: Trajectory Analysis :: Install the optional PyTorch/mlcolvar environment for the DeepTICA signal
ChimeraX :: Tool :: MDSmooth :: Trajectory Analysis :: Build a smoothed overlay of a trajectory and pop up its RMSD analysis
